content.problemStatement:
Now we’ll convert your **Notes API** into **Interface-Based Design** using proper layered architecture. \
We will create:
```json
Controller → Service (interface + impl) → Repository (interface + impl)
```
This is the **standard Spring Boot industry structure**.

---

**How Project Structure looks in this Example:**
```sss
com.example.demo
│
├── controller
│   └── NoteController.java
│
├── service
│   ├── NoteService.java          (INTERFACE)
│   └── NoteServiceImpl.java      (IMPLEMENTATION)
│
├── repository
│   ├── NoteRepository.java         (INTERFACE)
│   └── InMemoryNoteRepository.java (IMPLEMENTATION)
```

---

**1. NoteRepository (Interface)**
```java
public interface NoteRepository {
    void save(String note);
    List findAll();
}
```
**What this code does :**
- This is a repository contract.
- It defines the operations related to data storage.
- It does not contain any implementation logic.

**Methods**
- `save(String note)` → used to store a note
- `findAll()` → used to retrieve all notes

Any repository class must implement these methods.

---

**2. InMemoryNoteRepository (Repository Implementation)**
```java
@Repository
public class InMemoryNoteRepository implements NoteRepository {
```
**What this class does**
- Provides the **actual implementation** of the repository interface.
- Stores notes using an in-memory list.

**In-memory storage**
```java
private final List notes = new ArrayList<>();
```
- Creates a list to store notes temporarily.
- Data exists only while the application is running.

**Save method**
```java
@Override
public void save(String note) {
    notes.add(note);
}
```
Adds the received note into the list.

**Fetch method**
```java
@Override
public List findAll() {
    return notes;
}
```
Returns all stored notes.

**Annotation :**
```java
@Repository
```
- Marks this class as a repository component.
- Spring automatically creates and manages its object.

---

**3. NoteService (Interface)**
```java
public interface NoteService {
    void addNote(String note);
    List getAllNotes();
}
```
**What this code does**
- Defines business operations related to notes.
- Contains only method declarations.
- No logic is written here.

**Methods**
- `addNote()` → adds a note
- `getAllNotes()` → retrieves notes

---

**4. NoteServiceImpl (Service Implementation)**
```java
@Service
public class NoteServiceImpl implements NoteService {
```
**What this class does**
- Contains the **business logic** of the application.
- Uses repository methods to perform data operations.

**Dependency declaration**
```java
private final NoteRepository noteRepository;
```
- Service depends on the repository interface.
- The actual implementation is injected by Spring.

**Constructor Injection**
```java
public NoteServiceImpl(NoteRepository noteRepository) {
    this.noteRepository = noteRepository;
}
```
- Spring automatically provides a repository object.
- No manual object creation is required.

**Add note logic**
```java
@Override
public void addNote(String note) {
    noteRepository.save(note);
}
```
Calls repository to store the note.

**Get notes logic**
```java
@Override
public List getAllNotes() {
    return noteRepository.findAll();
}
```
Retrieves notes from repository.

**Annotation**
```java
@Service
```
- Marks this class as a service layer component.
- Spring manages its lifecycle automatically.

---

**5. NoteController**
```java
@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api/notes")
```
**What this class does**
- Handles HTTP requests.
- Communicates with the service layer.
- Does not contain business or storage logic.

**Service dependency**
```java
private final NoteService noteService;
```
Controller uses the service interface.

**Constructor Injection**
```java
public NoteController(NoteService noteService) {
    this.noteService = noteService;
}
```
Spring injects the service implementation automatically.


**POST Endpoint — Add Note**
```java
@PostMapping
public String addNote(@RequestBody String note)
```
- Receives note text from request body.
- Calls service to add the note.
- Returns success message.

**GET Endpoint — Get Notes**
```java
@GetMapping("/list")
public List getNotes()
```
- Calls service to fetch all notes.
- Returns notes as JSON response.
